title: Your Own Account, Your Own Decisions
subtitle: A visualisation for picturing a version of your financial life where autonomy and partnership sit comfortably side by side
excerpt: A short visualisation for the stuck-on feeling of financial smallness within a partnership. The image is not independence from your partner but financial selfhood alongside them, your account, your decisions, your money, and their support still present.

content.text: A short visualisation for reclaiming financial autonomy within a shared life. The image is not separation or independence from a partner. It is the version of financial life where both things are true at once.
Sit comfortably. Close your eyes or let your gaze soften.

Picture yourself looking at a bank account. It has your name on it. The balance is yours to decide with, not large, not small, just yours. You did not need to ask for it or justify it. It exists because you are a full participant in this financial life.

See yourself making a decision from that account. Something for your work, your pleasure, your future, or your present. You make it without apology. Without explaining yourself to anyone. Not because you are hiding it, but because it is yours to make.
Your partner is still there. The partnership is intact. The support is still present. Autonomy and love are not in competition here.
Sit with that picture. Notice how it feels to hold both things at once.
Open your eyes when you are ready.
